llm.description: Compact label for status, counts, or tags. Not clickable — if the affordance needs to trigger an action, use a small button instead. Tones align with the rest of the catalog (neutral/primary/success/warning/danger).

examples.caption: Solid primary tag for highlighted items.
Subtle success label in the larger size.

antiPatterns.reason: Badges are not interactive. Use UI.button with size=sm for a clickable chip.

antiPatterns.code: UI.badge("Download the Q3 2024 revenue and expenses report here", tone="primary")
antiPatterns.reason: Badge copy must be short (ideally ≤3 words). Long copy belongs in Text, not a badge.

fallback.notes: Basic renderers drop tone + variant + size styling; the badge collapses to a short plain-text run.

llm.description: An inline notification row for surface-level messages. Pick tone by urgency: info/success render with role=status (polite); warning/danger render with role=alert (assertive). Attach an optional `action` button for recovery flows and a `dismiss` button for transient messages.

examples.code: UI.banner("Heads up", "We're updating the pricing page at 5pm UTC.", tone="info")
examples.caption: Informational banner.

examples.code: UI.banner("Payment failed", "Your card was declined.", tone="danger", action=retry_btn, dismiss=close_btn)
examples.caption: Danger banner with retry + dismiss controls.

antiPatterns.reason: Stacking more than three banners on one surface drowns the signal. Use a list + counters instead.
tone=danger signals an error. Use tone=success for confirmations.

fallback.notes: Basic renderers drop tone + role + icon slot; the row stays but reads as plain text with no urgency affordance.

llm.description: A clickable button that dispatches a server action. Use tone=primary for the single call-to-action per surface; tone=danger for destructive operations; tone=neutral elsewhere.

antiPatterns.reason: Only one primary CTA per surface. Second button should be tone=neutral.
Every FluxButton requires a non-empty action.
tone=danger signals destructive intent. Don't use it for confirmations.

fallback.notes: Basic renderers lose size + emphasis + tone=success; degrades to primary or default variant only. Labels are wrapped in a generated Text child at compile time.

llm.description: A surface container with optional header, required body, and optional footer. Pick `emphasis=subtle` for secondary content, `outline` for delineated groups, `elevated` for prominent callouts. Children are referenced by id — never inline them.

examples.caption: Outline card with header + body + footer action row.

antiPatterns.reason: Cards inside cards creates visual noise. Use a Stack or FluxStack for grouping within a single card.

fallback.notes: Basic renderers drop emphasis + padding tokens; the column stacks its slot children top-to-bottom with no surface styling.

llm.description: An inline hyperlink. Set `href` for navigation *or* `action` for an auth-gated / custom dispatch — never both. Use `external=True` for off-surface URLs so the renderer adds the external-link affordance and `rel=noopener`.

examples.code: UI.link("Sign in to continue", action="open_auth_modal", tone="default")
examples.caption: Action-based link that triggers a server event instead of navigating.

antiPatterns.reason: A link has exactly one destination. Pick either `href` (navigate) or `action` (dispatch).
Empty `href` renders a broken anchor. Use `action` when there is no URL.

fallback.notes: Basic catalog has no Link component; renderers degrade to inline Text with the label visible and the destination lost. The fluent guard warns when a FluxLink falls back to basic.

llm.description: Renders a Markdown source string as a prose block. The renderer sanitises HTML. Use `proseStyle=compact` for dense copy (tooltips, help), `default` for body text, `spacious` for long-form articles. Reach for plain `UI.text` for one-line labels — Markdown is overhead you don't need.

examples.code: UI.markdown("**Tip:** press Cmd+K to open the quick-switcher.")
examples.caption: Short inline help with bold + code formatting.

antiPatterns.reason: Raw HTML (especially scripts) must be stripped by the renderer. Don't rely on Markdown to pass HTML through; it won't.
Use UI.text for single-word labels. Markdown adds a parsing step with no payoff.

fallback.notes: Basic renderers lose markdown formatting; raw source is shown as plain text.

llm.description: A progress indicator. Set `determinate=True` with a `value` in 0..100 when you know the ratio; set `determinate=False` for unknown-duration waits (the renderer animates a shimmer). Always provide `accessibility.label` — assistive tech needs context ('Uploading photo', not just 'progress').

antiPatterns.reason: accessibility.label is required. Screen readers announce progress without any cue of what's progressing.
value must be in 0..100. Pass determinate=False for unknown-bounded progress.

fallback.notes: Basic renderers speak Slider — indeterminate mode and tone coloring are lost; the bar renders as a static slider pinned to `value`.

llm.description: Loading-state placeholder. Use `shape=text` for paragraph rows (combine with `count` to stack multiple lines), `shape=circle` for avatar placeholders, `shape=rect` for card/image placeholders. Always resolve to real content once data arrives — skeletons are transient.

antiPatterns.code: UI.column([UI.skeleton(shape="text")])   # never replaced with real content
antiPatterns.reason: Skeletons are loading states. Leaving one in place permanently confuses users; gate it on `is_loading` and swap in the real content when ready.

fallback.notes: Basic renderers have no shimmer primitive; the node degrades to an empty Text run. Users will see nothing — plan accordingly.

llm.description: Layout primitive for arranging children with consistent spacing. Pick `direction=horizontal` for toolbars / button rows and `vertical` for forms / lists. `gap` picks a step on the 4-px spacing grid. Children pass through — Stack has no slots.

antiPatterns.reason: Stack is for ≥2 children. A single child needs no wrapper — pass the component directly.
More than four nested stacks is a layout smell. Flatten or extract a Card / Column.

fallback.notes: Basic renderers only speak Column/Row — horizontal stacks degrade to a vertically stacked Column; gap/align/justify are dropped.

llm.description: A single-line text input. Use `type` to pick the semantic kind (email, password, number, …); use `state` to surface validation status. Always set `accessibility.label` — screen readers need it even when a visible placeholder exists.

examples.caption: Numeric field surfacing a validation error.

antiPatterns.reason: accessibility.label is required — placeholders are not a11y labels.
type=password hides input; reserve it for actual credentials, not long IDs the user may need to paste.

fallback.notes: Basic renderers drop size / state / helper / icons — only the raw input with a placeholder survives.
